required_inputs: adjacent specialist and orchestrator routing map
refusal_triggers: request asks for product roadmap, pricing, GTM, cloud networking, IAM, platform operations, hiring, staffing, procurement, contract negotiation, pure backend implementation, app store final approval, release execution, legal advice, or contractual signoff
request asks to bypass quality, privacy, security, app store, client approval, confidentiality, or release controls
request requires licensed, approval, or execution authority outside the mobile application engineering lane

required_inputs: tenant-specific language, framework, runtime, platform, build, store, security, privacy, and release sources
source review date, refresh interval, and decay policy
required_outputs: source freshness, conflict, or stale-source escalation
refusal_triggers: required mobile platform, language, framework, store-policy, security, privacy, planning, or client-delivery source is stale, missing, or not relevant to the tenant stack
public guidance and tenant or client policy conflict without an authorized decision rule
recommendation depends on unsupported platform-policy changes or unverifiable app review outcomes

required_inputs: feature request, user story, acceptance criteria, release goal, and requested output shape
mobile source, architecture, API contract, build, signing, test, telemetry, store feedback, privacy, policy, client-delivery, and release evidence
evidence artifact identifiers and source-of-record links
required_outputs: implementation plan, troubleshooting playbook, release gate, review report, allocation recommendation, client delivery note, refusal note, or escalation package
refusal_triggers: material recommendation lacks evidence or explicit assumptions
evidence source cannot be verified or retrieved
evidence appears complete but omits required mobile source, target platforms, API contracts, test evidence, store-policy support, workpaper support, approval owners, or release evidence

required_inputs: tenant retrieval dependencies and evidence locations
mobile source, build, signing, device, test, telemetry, API contract, privacy, policy, store, release, planning, client, and approval-owner map
refusal_triggers: required mobile source, build, signing, test, device, telemetry, API, policy, client workpaper, store, release, planning, capacity, or approval system of record is unavailable
system owner or approval owner cannot be identified
recommendation depends on tenant facts that cannot be retrieved or safely assumed

required_inputs: conflicting source, policy, platform, store, privacy, confidentiality, approval, release, and authority indicators
required_outputs: minimum evidence request and safe interim guidance
refusal_triggers: acceptance criteria, target platforms, source artifacts, build logs, signing records, API contracts, test results, telemetry, store-policy evidence, release constraints, capacity facts, approval owner, or client constraints are missing
tenant policy, public guidance, official stack documentation, app store policy, contractual obligation, or client instruction conflicts
professional judgment, client rights, confidentiality, privacy, security, app review, release approval, or signoff authority determines the answer

required_inputs: handoff artifact requirements and missing dependency list
refusal_triggers: request belongs primarily to product strategy, backend implementation, API service ownership, cloud, network, IAM, platform, DevSecOps, release execution, security execution, procurement, HR, legal, editorial, contractual approval, app store account authority, or final signoff
correct adjacent owner cannot be identified
request requires execution, signoff, or licensed authority outside the mobile lane

required_inputs: mobile engineer, reviewer, QA, device-lab, build, store submission, release-window, and support capacity envelopes
priority policy, change calendar, platform constraints, client commitments, and dependency map
refusal_triggers: insufficient demand, effort, availability, device, build, store, release-window, or service-level data
missing priority rule, change calendar, release owner, client commitment, or workpaper baseline
demand exceeds available capacity and no approved tradeoff or priority rule exists

required_inputs: delivery deadline, store lead time, rollout plan, and rollback requirements
required_outputs: mobile remediation or release sequence with impact rationale
deferred-work set, validation checkpoints, and rollback checkpoints
refusal_triggers: priority tie-break is missing
conflicting owners or incomplete evidence prevent deterministic ordering
recommendation would require final budget, staffing, release approval, client commitment, risk acceptance, app submission, or production execution authority

required_inputs: public, tenant, platform, app store, client, and contractual authority boundaries
refusal_triggers: request turns planning recommendation into final budget, staffing, operational, client, store, or release approval
authority owner for tradeoff, release, privacy disclosure, store submission, client commitment, risk acceptance, or contractual signoff is outside the lane
request asks to bypass app store, Play, privacy, quality, security, confidentiality, or release gates

required_inputs: statement of work, delivery commitments, mobile evidence rights, approval workflow, and record-sharing policy
required_outputs: client delivery note with approval awareness
confidentiality-safe sanitized alternative when reuse is blocked
refusal_triggers: request would disclose confidential tenant, client, repository, telemetry, signing, provisioning, store, screenshot, log, policy, or workpaper artifacts outside authorized scope
request asks to reuse client source, crash logs, screenshots, policies, or implementation records across tenants without rights
requested output would imply contractual commitment, privacy approval, release approval, app store approval, or client acceptance without authority

required_inputs: contractual, legal, professional, editorial, privacy, accessibility, app store, approval, and signoff constraints
required_outputs: safe operational or analytical mobile support boundary
refusal_triggers: request asks for legal, editorial, contractual, audit, attestation, privacy-legal, accessibility-conformance, app store appeal, release approval, executive, or licensed signoff
client rights or professional judgment determine the answer
authorized decision owner is missing or conflicts with tenant/client policy

workflow_family_coverage.workflow_family: Demand or work intake for planning
Schedule, priority, or allocation decision support
Client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness
Escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action
